title: Mildly Smoky Kenyan Lentil Dal
description: A resinous and crisp Kenyan dish featuring brown lentils and a hint of smoke, perfect for a satvik-friendly meal. This dish is made with aromatic spices and smoked paprika to give it a unique flavor.
instructions: Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat and add cumin seeds to let them sizzle.
Add chopped onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for another minute.
Add smoked paprika, coriander powder, turmeric, and salt, and stir well.
Add brown lentils and water to the pan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and let the dal simmer for 30 minutes or until the lentils are tender.
Use an immersion blender to partially blend the dal and give it a thick consistency.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the dal hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro.
